core.action: Assume the framework validates signal-to-execution timing within each bar — the framework does not track bar-level signal-execution timing; orders may execute in incorrect time contexts

consequence.description: Without bar-level timing validation, signals and executions may be misaligned, causing incorrect backtest fills and distorted performance metrics

core.action: Track timing metadata for each bar: record signal generation timestamp, order submission timestamp, and verify execution occurs within the same bar or next valid bar per strategy rules

core.when: When calculating transaction costs and slippage in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework includes each cost components in backtesting — the cost model is incomplete; slippage, market impact, funding rates, or other cost factors may be missing

consequence.description: Incomplete cost models cause backtest returns to overestimate actual trading profitability, leading to strategies that appear profitable but lose money live

core.when: When configuring transaction cost model for backtesting
core.action: Document each cost components used in the backtest: commission rates, slippage assumptions, spread costs, market impact estimates, and funding rates; verify alignment with actual broker or exchange fee schedules

consequence.description: Explicit cost documentation ensures backtest accuracy matches live trading costs, preventing strategy selection based on overstated backtest returns

core.when: When processing positions or calculating portfolio costs in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework handles carry/funding cost modeling — the framework does not implement carry cost calculations for long positions or funding cost calculations for short positions

consequence.description: Without carry/funding cost modeling, backtest results exclude financing costs, systematically overstating portfolio returns especially for long-short strategies and margin trading scenarios where costs significantly impact net performance

evidence_refs.summary: 0f9738a Derived from BD: BD-GAP-015

core.when: When implementing position or portfolio cost calculations in backtesting
core.action: Implement carry/funding cost calculations using position size, borrow rate for short positions, dividend yield for long positions, and holding period duration to model actual financing costs

consequence.description: Omitting carry cost calculations causes backtest results to exclude financing charges, leading to performance overestimation of 50-200 basis points annually for leveraged strategies

core.action: Assume the framework provides CRD (Credit Risk) score functionality — CRD scoring is explicitly not in scope; this is a wealth management platform, not a credit risk system

consequence.description: Assuming CRD score availability will cause the backtest to fail at runtime or produce misleading risk assessments that do not reflect the platform's actual credit risk handling capabilities

core.action: Verify that credit risk scoring (CRD) is not required for the strategy — if CRD scores are needed, they must be sourced from an external credit risk service; do not assume the framework provides this functionality

consequence.description: Strategies that rely on CRD scoring will produce incomplete risk assessments or fail silently, leading to underestimation of portfolio risk in live trading

core.when: When configuring multiple data providers in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework handles provider priority ranking or credential isolation between data providers — this functionality is explicitly missing and requires manual implementation

consequence.description: Without provider priority handling, the backtest may consume lower-quality data when higher-quality sources are available, distorting backtest results; credential mixing could cause data leakage between providers

evidence_refs.summary: 0f9738a Derived from BD: BD-GAP-030

core.when: When implementing multi-provider data collection in backtesting
core.action: Implement explicit provider priority ranking logic (e.g., priority_score field per provider) and isolate provider credentials using separate credential stores per provider — never mix credentials across providers

consequence.description: Missing provider priority and credential isolation will cause data quality degradation when providers fail and potential credential leakage, leading to unreliable backtest results and security vulnerabilities
